content.problemStatement:
There are NNN mountains in Chefland, arranged in a line and numbered 111 through NNN from left to right. For each valid iii, the iii-th mountain from the left has a height HiH_iHi‚Äč.

Chef wants to choose a contiguous sequence of mountains l,l+1,‚Ä¶,rl, l+1, \ldots, rl,l+1,‚Ä¶,r and visit them in this order. He has a notebook where he writes down some heights of mountains. Initially, the notebook contains only the integer 000 and each time he visits a new mountain whose height is strictly greater than all the integers written in the notebook so far, he writes its height in the notebook. The satisfaction Chef gets from travelling is the number of positive integers written in the notebook at the end. For example, if the mountains have heights (1‚Äĺ,5‚Äĺ,2,5,3,7‚Äĺ,2,9‚Äĺ)(\underline{1}, \underline{5}, 2, 5, 3, \underline{7}, 2, \underline{9})(1‚Äč,5‚Äč,2,5,3,7‚Äč,2,9‚Äč), Chef's satisfaction is 444 and the integers written in the notebook at the end are 0,1,5,7,90, 1, 5, 7, 90,1,5,7,9.

Your task is to answer QQQ queries. In each query, Chef gives you two integers LLL and RRR (1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N1 \le L \le R \le N1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N) and you should find the maximum satisfaction he can get if he can choose to visit any contiguous sequence of mountains from lll to rrr such that L‚Č§l‚Č§r‚Č§RL \le l \le r \le RL‚Č§l‚Č§r‚Č§R. Note that the queries are encoded in such a way that they have to be processed online.
constraints: **Subtask #2 (35 points):** s=0s=0s=0, i.e. the queries do not have to be processed online

`ExplanationIn the first query, Chef can choose to visit a contiguous subsequence of mountains with heights (4,2,3,1,5,5)(4, 2, 3, 1, 5, 5)(4,2,3,1,5,5). The subsequence that gives the maximum satisfaction is (2,3,1,5)(2, 3, 1, 5)(2,3,1,5). In that case, Chef writes the integers 0,2,3,50, 2, 3, 50,2,3,5 to his notebook, so the answer is 333.

In the second query, Chef can choose to visit a contiguous subsequence of mountains with heights (2,3,1)(2, 3, 1)(2,3,1). The whole sequence has the maximum satisfaction 222, since Chef writes the integers 0,2,30, 2, 30,2,3.
In the third query, the largest (and the only possible) satisfaction is 111, since Chef writes either 0,30, 30,3 or 0,10, 10,1.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, QQQ and sss.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers H1,H2,‚Ä¶,HNH_1, H_2, \ldots, H_NH1‚Äč,H2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,HN‚Äč.
Each of the following QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers xxx and yyy describing a query. The parameters LLL and RRR for this query can be computed as follows:

Let lastlastlast be the answer to the previous query, or 000 if this is the first query in this test case.
Set LLL to (x+s‚čÖlast‚ąí1)‚ÄČ%‚ÄČN+1(x + s \cdot last - 1) \,\%\, N + 1(x+s‚čÖlast‚ąí1)%N+1.
Set RRR to (y+s‚čÖlast‚ąí1)‚ÄČ%‚ÄČN+1(y + s \cdot last - 1) \,\%\, N + 1(y+s‚čÖlast‚ąí1)%N+1.
If L>RL \gt RL>R, swap LLL and RRR.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the maximum satisfaction Chef can get.
